how_computed: Percentage of gold (endpoint x scenario) cases where the agent's harness-observed token equals the gold observed token. A scenario the agent did not exercise (request not emitted -> observed 'missing') counts as a mismatch. Denominator = all gold scenarios (endpoints x 5).
headline_definition: IP Allowlist Enforcement Rate = (requests correctly allowed or blocked per the current allowlist / total IP enforcement test cases) x 100. Pass = 100% (every allowlisted source IP gets exactly 200 with data, every non-allowlisted source IP and every X-Forwarded-For spoof gets exactly 403 with no resource data, and allowlist add/remove updates take effect). Fail = ANY non-allowlisted IP receiving 200 (critical, P1) OR any X-Forwarded-For spoof producing 200 (equally critical) OR any 403 leaking resource data.
rationale: All four agents hit the same local ip-allowlist-gateway, whose four COMPLIANT endpoints enforce correctly and whose two VULNERABLE endpoints honor a spoofed X-Forwarded-For. So the raw IP Allowlist Enforcement Rate is identical for every agent (~93.33%: the two xff_spoof_rejected cases return 200 instead of 403) and is a property of the target, not the agent — it is the task's QA finding, emitted by every agent as the critical P1 bypass. Fidelity-to-gold instead measures each framework's TEST quality: did it construct the correct five-case sequence (allowlisted baseline, non-allowlisted baseline, the X-Forwarded-For spoof, the allowlist-add, and the allowlist-remove) and faithfully exercise every case so its observed token reproduces the gold token — including correctly OBSERVING the bypass on the vulnerable endpoints rather than missing or mis-constructing it.
pass_threshold: fidelity = 100% (agent reproduces every gold observed token, including the seeded bypass observations)
fail_condition: fidelity < 100% (any scenario uncovered or any request/management action mis-constructed so the observed token diverges from gold)
